titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Phillip T. Young Correspondence and Photographs
titlestmt.author: Tina Evans

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 23:59

did.unittitle: Phillip T. Young correspondence and photographs

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Peter Harrington on the Danford F. Barney fund, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Oragnized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1942 - 1961. II. Photographs, 1930 - 1946. III. Printed material, 1943.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Phillip Taylor Young (1926-2002) was a naturalized Canadian musician, teacher, and the author of books on woodwind and reed instruments and other related topics. Born in Milton, Massachusetts, Young served in World War II before studying music at Bowdoin College and Yale University (M MUS 1962). He taught at the Yale School of Music, the Taft School, and the University of Victoria.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Phillip T. Young Correspondence and Photographs. James Weldon Johnson Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, photographs, and printed material documenting Phillip T. Young's interest in jazz as a teenager. Correspondence includes letters from Theodore Carpenter, Rex Stewart, and Earle Warren. Collection also contains photographs - many of which are signed and inscribed to Phillip T. Young - of the Count Basie Orchestra, the Duke Ellington, Orchestra, as well as photographs of individual members and other performers such as Thelma Carpenter and printed material including programs from jazz clubs.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Phillip T. Young Correspondence and photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed by members of the Orchestra and inscribed to "Harry."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographer's stamp on verso: Harry Rossner. Accompanied by manuscript identification in unidentified hand.

did.unittitle: Duke Ellington Orchestra Cabin in the Sky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One photograph is signed by members of the Orchestra.

did.unittitle: Sam Nanton, Harry Carney, Harold Baker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a Hurricane Club folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a Three Deuces folder.

did.unittitle: Café Zanzibar program and matchbook cover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One clipping is signed by Count Basie and Buck Clayton.

did.unittitle: Duke Ellington and his famous orchestra: program
Duke Ellington and his Orchestra Swing concert Symphony Hall Boston: program
Jazz Ellingtonia issue Vol. 1, nos. 5 and 6
